message:
Fix analytics dashboard layout, remove debug rendering, and schedule transition cleanup ticks

The dashboard layout had several issues:
- A debug paragraph rendering agent count/bar_rows/has_bar was left in the
  analytics bar chart code path, cluttering the UI with red debug text.
- The sparkline section used Min constraints that could over-allocate space;
  switching to Fixed(2) for the sparkline (label + bars) with Min(0) for
  remaining space ensures deterministic layout.
- Bars for non-zero agent token values could render at 0-width when the
  ratio was very small; adding .max(1.0) guarantees visibility.
- The sparkline now renders a "Daily Tokens (N days)" label above the bars
  when at least 2 rows are available, improving readability.
- The breakdowns content area is now inset by 1 column to leave a gutter
  for the selection indicator arrow.

In app.rs, surface and analytics-view transition functions now return
Cmd::tick scheduled slightly after the transition duration, so the
transition overlay is reliably cleared. Previously, transitions could
remain stuck if no other events arrived to trigger a re-render. All call
sites now propagate transition commands via Cmd::batch when combined with
other async work (e.g. analytics reload, search drilldown).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
